name: Woosuk Kwon
bio: Woosuk Kwon is the co-founder and CTO of Inferact, an AI inference infrastructure startup that launched in January 2026 with $150M in seed funding at an $800M valuation led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed. He co-created vLLM, the most widely adopted open-source LLM inference engine with over 2,000 contributors, and invented PagedAttention, an algorithm inspired by OS virtual memory that eliminates 60-80% of GPU memory waste in KV caches. Kwon earned dual B.S. degrees in Computer Science and Mathematical Sciences from Seoul National University (ranked 1st of 134 students) and a Ph.D. in Computer Science from UC Berkeley under Ion Stoica with a 4.0 GPA, completing his dissertation in December 2025. Before founding Inferact, he was a Research Scientist at Google DeepMind (2024-2025) and a Member of Technical Staff at Thinking Machines Lab (May-Nov 2025).

timeline.event: Began dual B.S. in Computer Science and Mathematical Sciences at Seoul National University
Published 'Nimble: Lightweight and Parallel GPU Task Scheduling for Deep Learning' at NeurIPS 2020
Published 'Graphene: Strong yet Lightweight Row Hammer Protection' at MICRO 2020
Graduated from Seoul National University; began Ph.D. at UC Berkeley under Ion Stoica
Published 'Learned Token Pruning for Transformers' at KDD 2022 and post-training pruning framework at NeurIPS 2022
Received a16z Open Source AI Grant for vLLM
vLLM deployed at LMSYS Chatbot Arena and Vicuna Demo, handling 30K daily requests
Publicly announced vLLM with PagedAttention, achieving 24x throughput over HuggingFace Transformers
Published 'SkyPilot: An Intercloud Broker for Sky Computing' at NSDI 2023
Published 'Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention' at SOSP 2023
Selected as Sequoia Open Source Fellow
Joined Google DeepMind as Research Scientist
Presented 'vLLM: Easy, Fast, and Cheap LLM Serving for Everyone' at PyTorch Conference 2024
Published 'Jenga: Effective Memory Management for Serving LLM with Heterogeneity' at SOSP 2025
Joined Thinking Machines Lab as Member of Technical Staff
Co-founded Inferact with Ion Stoica and other vLLM core maintainers
Completed Ph.D. at UC Berkeley; dissertation: 'vLLM: An Efficient Inference Engine for Large Language Models'
Inferact launched publicly with $150M seed funding at $800M valuation, led by a16z and Lightspeed

key_contributions.title: vLLM
key_contributions.description: The most widely adopted open-source LLM inference and serving engine, achieving 24x throughput over HuggingFace Transformers and 3.5x over TGI. Over 2,000 contributors and used by major AI labs globally.

key_contributions.title: PagedAttention
key_contributions.description: Novel attention algorithm inspired by OS virtual memory paging that partitions KV caches into non-contiguous blocks, reducing memory waste from 60-80% to under 4% and enabling copy-on-write memory sharing across requests.

key_contributions.title: Inferact
key_contributions.description: AI inference infrastructure company commercializing vLLM with a serverless managed offering, observability, and multi-hardware support. Raised $150M seed at $800M valuation from a16z, Lightspeed, Sequoia, and Databricks.

key_contributions.title: Jenga (SOSP 2025)
key_contributions.description: Memory management system for serving LLMs with heterogeneous hardware, extending PagedAttention principles to mixed GPU environments.

key_contributions.title: SkyPilot (NSDI 2023)
key_contributions.description: Intercloud broker for sky computing that optimizes workload placement across multiple cloud providers for cost and performance.

key_contributions.title: Learned Token Pruning (KDD 2022)
key_contributions.description: Method for pruning tokens in Transformer models to accelerate inference while maintaining accuracy.

key_contributions.title: Nimble (NeurIPS 2020)
key_contributions.description: Lightweight and parallel GPU task scheduling framework for deep learning workloads.

quotes.text: We see a future where serving AI becomes effortless. Today, deploying a frontier model at scale requires a dedicated infrastructure team. Tomorrow, it should be as simple as spinning up a serverless database. The complexity doesn't disappear; it gets absorbed into the infrastructure we're building.
vLLM taught me a tough lesson: to keep the GPU fully utilized, we need to pay close attention to everything happening on the CPU.
vLLM improves the throughput of popular LLMs by 2-4x with the same level of latency compared to the state-of-the-art systems, such as FasterTransformer and Orca.
